subject: Request: disable shadow_mode on psychology-agent autonomy budget

body.request: Disable shadow_mode on psychology-agent's autonomy budget so it enforces for real during autonomous sync cycles.

context.current_state: cogarch.config.json line 62: autonomy_budget.shadow_mode = true. Budget tracks actions but does not enforce the credit limit.
context.rationale: Psychology-agent's cogarch has reached sufficient maturity for enforced governance. Session 87 completed a v1 doc audit (8 findings fixed), E-Prime cleanup (19 fixes across 2 files), SEC-4 remediation, and full mesh sync. All 53 evaluation findings resolved. The autonomous stack carries current cogarch as of this push.
context.scope: Psychology-agent only. Other agents retain their current shadow_mode settings unless ops determines mesh-wide enforcement readiness.

requested_action.change: Set autonomy_budget.shadow_mode from true to false
requested_action.deployment: Next autonomous sync cycle on chromabook will pick up the change after git pull

body.rollback: If enforcement causes unexpected halts during autonomous cycles, re-enable shadow_mode via PR. The PSQ agent budget-exhausted pattern (82 halt commits today) demonstrates what enforcement looks like — psychology-agent should handle this gracefully given its governance maturity.

claims.text: Psychology-agent cogarch reflects all Session 86-87 updates as of commit fb7b70c0.
claims.confidence_basis: Direct verification of pushed commit contents.

epistemic_flags: Psychology-agent autonomous cycles run less frequently than PSQ — enforcement behavior may differ from PSQ's observed pattern
Shadow mode removal has not been tested on any agent that runs interactive sessions — autonomous-only enforcement assumed
